Psilocybe is a genus of fungi, commonly known as magic mushrooms, that contains psychoactive compounds and is often used in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Psilocybe are primarily related to its potential to reduce symptoms of depression, anxiety, and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). The bioactive constituents of Psilocybe include psilocybin, psilocin, and other tryptamines, which contribute to its psychoactive properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Psilocybe are dried mushroom capsules, tea infusions, and extracts, although the use and cultivation of these mushrooms are heavily regulated in many countries.
